What's up everyone? So step two in this funnilology is image compression. So I've done this a few times and the easiest way to do it is to pull up the image that you actually want. Now, if you have a really high res image, the best thing to do is pull up the actual image itself and then shrink it all the way down to what you want it to be. So itty bitty bitty, as small as you can go and then take a screenshot of that image and then it'll only give you the pixels that are in the screenshot. So it'll actually still come out fine for most people if most of your traffic is digital or mobile, excuse me. This will come out really well. Now, in this instance, I know that this one has already been compressed but we can assume that it hasn't for the purpose of this demonstration. And then we go over to imagecompressor.com. So that's the free tool that I use. It works really well and it's free, which is always great. You're going to click upload files. I think that one was 5560 and here we are. So this is 190 kilobytes, which is a great size to start with. Typically, you'll get like a 76% reduction in size. Now, this one, I think I've actually already reduced this image before so I should probably try and find one that I haven't. That would make sense because that's why it is so small already. Let me see if I can find a really good one. So normally, you'll be able to compress these guys from a much bigger image to a much smaller one. You'll usually get like 70 or 80% knocked off of this. I wish I had a high-rise image. I just compressed like almost everything. Let's see how big this guy is. Let's go view... Get info. Okay, so this is 1.7 megabytes. So let's upload this guy. So this is screenshots. We're going to copy that and then we're going to search for it. So let's get this guy out of here. Let's put this guy in here. So you can see 1.6 megabytes becomes like a 70% reduction and then you just download it. Really, really easy. And then you'll get a much smaller version which in this case is 500 or so. I usually repeat this process a couple of times, believe it or not. I try and get all my images under 100 kb if I can. So even this one right here, I want to see if I can compress this guy even further. So this is really, really small and so I want to go even smaller than this because they only got me 4% on this guy. So I want to see if I can get this to be itty bitty bitty. So let's go here again. Let's upload this file. And let's go to desktop and let's just pretend that things decided to work my way. So yeah, so this one's 244. Now we're going to start here and see how this one goes down. Right, so now I went from 244 and I'm going to download this guy. Now let's look at this one. And so if I'm going to show you, I know this sounds like details but it's the details that wins the wars here. So this one just happened. So right here you can see this one's at 67 kb. And so we were able to compress this thing down and this was a super high-rise image that was multiple megabytes. And having gone through this conversion process, it still looks just about as good as it before but it's almost like 100th of the size. And this is how you can have lots of images on a page and have it not affect the load time. So this is probably one of the most used tools that I have in my arsenal whenever I'm building any kind of funnel. This is the primary thing that I use to compress image sizes. Alright, so that's the first one that I wanted to show you.
